config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena justru hak individu adalah batas agar kekuasaan tidak semena-mena. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat kehidupan sosial di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” kadang terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan ketidakadilan atau membungkam suara yang lemah. Masyarakat yang sehat seharusnya mencari keseimbangan: kepentingan umum dilindungi tanpa mengorbankan martabat dan kebebasan dasar tiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter a lot, but they should not automatically override individual rights. From my perspective as someone from China, I have seen how strong collective values can help people cooperate and endure hardship, yet I also think “for the public good” is too easily used to excuse unfair treatment or silence ordinary people. A society is not truly healthy if it sacrifices basic dignity, fairness, and personal protection whenever the majority or authorities claim a broader benefit. The right balance is to protect society while keeping clear limits on power, so individual rights are not treated as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Sebagai orang Indonesia, saya melihat slogan “demi kepentingan bersama” sering dipakai untuk menekan yang lemah, menutup kritik, atau membenarkan kebijakan yang tidak adil, jadi saya tidak percaya hak individu boleh dianggap sekunder begitu saja. Kepentingan umum memang penting, terutama untuk hal seperti keselamatan, ketertiban, dan keadilan sosial, tetapi itu harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar agar negara atau mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi keduanya sekaligus.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically come before individual rights, because that creates too much room for abuse. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand the appeal of collective priority: people can cooperate better, endure hardship, and build stability when everyone is asked to contribute. But I also know that appeals to the common good can become a convenient excuse to ignore fairness, silence criticism, or treat ordinary people as expendable. A good society should protect the public interest while setting firm limits, because once basic rights are weakened, the society itself becomes less trustworthy and less healthy.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Di Indonesia, saya terlalu sering melihat dalih “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk menekan yang lemah, membungkam kritik, atau melegitimasi ketidakadilan, jadi saya curiga kalau hak individu dianggap nomor dua. Memang ada situasi darurat seperti keselamatan publik atau ketertiban, tetapi batasnya harus jelas supaya negara dan mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat justru yang bisa melindungi kepentingan bersama tanpa mengorbankan martabat dan kebebasan dasar tiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not be placed above individual rights as a general rule, because that gives too much power to those who claim to speak for the common good. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why collective priority feels natural: social stability, cooperation, and shared sacrifice can help a country survive difficult times. But I also think the phrase “for society” is too easily used to justify unfairness, silence criticism, or treat ordinary people as if their dignity matters less. A healthy society should protect the public good and individual rights at the same time, with clear limits on power, not assume one can always cancel the other.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
